capability_questions.capability_name: Service exists and is in good condition

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have a log management service?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether the enterprise has a log management service.
questions.evaluation_focus: Enterprise log management service exists

questions.prompt: Is the log management service reviewed frequently for efficacy?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether the log management service is reviewed frequently for efficacy.
questions.evaluation_focus: Frequent log management service efficacy review

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise formalize and document all KPIs and KRIs for the Log Management?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether KPIs and KRIs for log management are formalized and documented.
questions.evaluation_focus: Documented KPIs and KRIs for log management

questions.prompt: Are the KPIs and KRIs for the Log Management reviewed frequently?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether log management KPIs and KRIs are reviewed frequently.
questions.evaluation_focus: Frequent KPI/KRI review

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have a logging system to manage and correlate all logs?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether a logging system manages and correlates all logs.
questions.evaluation_focus: Logging system to manage and correlate all logs

questions.prompt: Does the logging system consume raw logs from all segments of the enterprise?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether the logging system consumes raw logs from all enterprise segments.
questions.evaluation_focus: Raw log consumption from all enterprise segments

questions.prompt: Does the logging system implement automation to process large numbers of logs?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether the logging system automates processing of large log volumes.
questions.evaluation_focus: Automation to process large volumes of logs

questions.prompt: Does the logging system configuration get reviewed frequently to maintain efficacy?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether logging system configuration is reviewed frequently.
questions.evaluation_focus: Frequent logging system configuration review

questions.prompt: Does the logging system integrate with various other segments like compliance, vulnerability, and asset management to provide contextual information?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether the logging system integrates with compliance, vulnerability, and asset management.
questions.evaluation_focus: Integration with compliance, vulnerability, and asset management

questions.prompt: Do all identity technology send identity and access logs automatically to a centralized logging entity?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether all identity technology sends identity and access logs automatically to centralized logging.
questions.evaluation_focus: Automatic centralized identity and access logging

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise logging solution have capability to monitor and identify anomalies?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether the logging solution monitors and identifies anomalies.
questions.evaluation_focus: Anomaly monitoring and identification capability

questions.prompt: Can responses be configured to be automated?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether anomaly responses can be configured to be automated.
questions.evaluation_focus: Configurable automated anomaly responses

questions.prompt: Do all device technology send device and access logs automatically to a centralized logging entity?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether all endpoint device technology sends logs automatically to centralized logging.
questions.evaluation_focus: Automatic centralized endpoint device and access logging

questions.prompt: Do all network technology send network and access logs automatically to a centralized logging entity?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether all network technology sends logs automatically to centralized logging.
questions.evaluation_focus: Automatic centralized network and access logging

questions.prompt: Do all application technology send application and access logs automatically to a centralized logging entity?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether all application technology sends logs automatically to centralized logging.
questions.evaluation_focus: Automatic centralized application and access logging

questions.prompt: Do all data asset technology send data and access logs automatically to a centralized logging entity?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether all data asset technology sends logs automatically to centralized logging.
questions.evaluation_focus: Automatic centralized data and access logging

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have a clear scope defined of which devices and technology will send logs to the logging server?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether a clear logging scope is defined for devices and technology.
questions.evaluation_focus: Clear defined logging scope for devices and technology

questions.prompt: Is the scope reevaluated frequently to maintain efficacy and stability of log system storage to prevent storage hog?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether logging scope is reevaluated frequently for efficacy and storage stability.
questions.evaluation_focus: Frequent scope reevaluation for efficacy and storage stability

questions.prompt: Does the logging solution produce audit logs for all technology that sends logs to it?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether the logging solution produces audit logs for all log-producing technology.
questions.evaluation_focus: Audit logs for all log-producing technology

questions.prompt: Does the log management system store all logs securely and prevent unauthorized changes to the logs?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether logs are stored securely and protected from unauthorized changes.
questions.evaluation_focus: Secure log storage and prevention of unauthorized log changes

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have a clear hierarchy of T1, T2, and T3 analysts to process logs?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether a clear T1, T2, and T3 analyst hierarchy processes logs.
questions.evaluation_focus: T1, T2, and T3 analyst hierarchy for log processing
